channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Military schools offer test case for Trump education reforms
item.description: Military academies could increasingly show what President Trump wants to see from public schools and colleges.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump team tries to project confidence and calm after his tariff moves rattled markets
item.description: White House advisers and Cabinet members are trying to project confidence and calm as they defend President Donald Trump’s economic policies after another week of reeling markets that saw the Republican administration reverse course on some of its steepest tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video of Trump allegedly 'snubbing' actress Cheryl Hines spreads like wildfire but doesn't show full picture
item.description: President Donald Trump circled back to greet Cheryl Hines despite recent headlines claiming the president snubbed Secretary Kennedy's wife at a UFC fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: State Department wants staff to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias during Biden's term
item.description: As the Christian world commemorates Holy Week leading up to Easter Sunday, the State Department has issued an appeal for its employees to report instances of alleged anti-Christian bias, including actions taken for opposition to vaccines or personal pronoun choice, that may have occurred during form
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bernie Sanders takes Coachella stage to rail against Trump, 'billionaire class' at high-dollar music festival
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders, of Vermont, ripped President Donald Trump and the "billionaire class" while introducing Clairo at the Coachella music festival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Americans witnessing crippling federal regulations can go directly to DOGE to report red tape rule
item.description: This DOGE-led effort highlights President Trump’s priority to put the people first and government bureaucrats last
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gretchen Whitmer ribbed online for seeming to hide face in Oval Office
item.description: Democratic Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer temporarily held up a folder to block her face from cameras in an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump goes with his gut and the world goes along for the ride
item.description: President Donald Trump likes keeping people on edge waiting for his next move, and that's been clear when it comes to his tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump sits cageside at Miami UFC event in his latest appearance at a sports event
item.description: President Donald Trump was in Miami for his latest appearance at a sports event as he watched UFC fighters and was joined by several Cabinet members and top administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump: Russia-Ukraine talks at 'put up or shut up' point
item.description: Trump's comments came after a top envoy traveled to Russia to meet directly with President Vladimir Putin on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Man deported to El Salvador 'alive and secure': Trump admin
item.description: Lawyers representing Abrego Garcia have proposed the federal government be held in contempt for prolonging efforts to have their client returned.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration says it will exclude some electronics from 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration says it will exclude electronics like smartphones and laptops from ‘reciprocal’ tariffs, a move that could help keep the prices down for popular consumer electronics that aren’t usually made in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Tim Scott outlines first 100 days of Congress, crypto, tariffs
item.description: Senate Banking Committee chairman Tim Scott described the first hundred days of Congress, a trade war with China and the timeline for implementing critical crypto policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration adds immigrant Social Security numbers to 'death list' in deportation push
item.description: It’s a move that may run afoul of SSA policy, as the Ineligible Master File is reserved for those who the agency knows are dead.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: K-12 schools face major Trump test on DEI demand
item.description: If the programs are not gone, the administration says, funding could be pulled from the K-12 districts.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico to make ‘immediate’ water payment to US as Trump threatens sanctions
item.description: Hours after President Donald Trump threatened Mexico with additional tariffs over a massive water debt, that country's president publicly vowed to make a substantial payment soon.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens sanctions, tariffs on Mexico in water dispute
item.description: Under a 1944 treaty, Mexico must send 1.75 million acre-feet of water to the U.S. from the Rio Grande.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about the Social Security Administration listing thousands of living immigrants as dead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration's move to classify thousands of living immigrants as dead and cancel their Social Security numbers is an escalation of the president's crackdown on people who were legally allowed to live in the U.S. under programs instituted by his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House responds to Supreme Court ruling on mistaken deportation
item.description: The White House on Friday responded to the Supreme Court’s ruling that the Trump administration must “facilitate” the return of a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rather than "effectuate" the return.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP ethics complaint alleges conflict by DFL Senate President Bobby Joe Champion
item.description: Six GOP lawmakers said the Minneapolis Democrat broke Senate rules when he carried legislation funding the nonprofit of a former legal client.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump nominee Caine for chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff in overnight vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine to become the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff on Friday, filling the position almost two months after President Donald Trump fired his predecessor. Trump nominated Caine to become the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Beijing slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods in latest U.S.-China trade escalation
item.description: China signals the latest tariff hike will be its last round of tit-for-tat measures, prompting sharp falls in European shares, as Asian stocks end the day mixed.
item.dc:creator: Sherisse Pham, MPR News

item.title: Democratic prosecutor in crime-torn Albuquerque launches bid for New Mexico governor
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A Democratic district attorney in the eye of New Mexico’s tempest over crime and accountability launched his candidacy for governor Thursday as the National Guard prepares to deploy to the state’s largest city to shore up public safety.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump administration must 'facilitate’ return of mistakenly deported man
item.description: The court’s emergency ruling instructs the lower judge to clarify the language of her order but said she acted properly in ensuring that Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s case is handled “as it would have been had he not been improperly sent to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal jobs watch: Just-released February jobs report shows few losses in Minnesota
item.description: The federal workforce includes nearly 34,000 jobs in Minnesota. Over 20,000 are located in the Twin Cities metropolitan area. The Bureau of Labor Statistics’ month jobs report shows a loss of 100 federal jobs through Minnesota from January to February.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Gawthrop and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: As measles spreads, federal budget cuts force closure of vaccine clinics
item.description: Federal funding cuts, though temporarily blocked by a judge, have upended vaccination outreach across the country, including in Arizona, Minnesota, Nevada, Texas, and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Bram Sable-Smith, Arielle Zionts, and Jackie Fortiér, MPR News

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
